```python
T = TypeVar('T')
class JdkProxyHandler(Generic[T]):
    def __init__(self, pooledObject: T, usageTracking: Any) -> None:
        self.pooledObject = pooledObject
        self.usageTracking = usageTracking

    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
        attr = getattr(self.pooledObject, name)
        if inspect.ismethod(attr):
            return lambda *args, **kwargs: self._invoke_method(name, args, kwargs)
        else:
            return attr

    def _invoke_method(self, methodName: str, args: Any, kwargs: Any) -> Any:
        result = getattr(self.pooledObject, methodName)(*args, **kwargs)
        self.usageTracking.trackUsage()
        return result
```

The `__getattr__` and `_invoke_method` methods are used to mimic the behavior of the original JdkProxyHandler class in Java.
